persona.name: Gregory Okonkwo
persona.background: Comfortable with formal processes and provides exactly the information requested. Reasonable and cooperative even when there's a problem to resolve.

user_goals: Check the current balance of his checking account ahead of his mortgage closing this week
Review recent transactions to find out why his balance dropped to almost nothing
Confirm the $58,000 he set aside for his down payment is actually still there
Get the balance corrected if the bank posted something in error
Make sure his funds are available in time for the closing on Friday
Get an official proof-of-funds letter he can give to the title company

tools.name: verify_customer_identity
tools.description: Verify customer identity using account number, SSN last 4, or security questions. Most account-accessing and state-changing tools require a successful identity verification first; call this before reading balances, transactions, or making any changes.

tools.name: get_account_balance
tools.description: Get current balance for a specific account. Requires verified identity. Resolve the customer-facing description (e.g. 'my checking account') to the correct account ID before calling.

tools.name: get_transaction_history
tools.description: Retrieve transaction history for an account. Requires verified identity. Use limit and date range to scope results; results are returned newest-first.

tools.name: submit_loan_application
tools.description: Submit a formal loan application for underwriting. Requires verified identity. This SUBMITS an application for review and credit decision; it does not approve or disburse a loan. Income is required for underwriting. Use get_interest_rates for indicative product rates before applying.

parameters.name: annual_income
parameters.description: Applicant's stated annual income in USD

tools.name: initiate_transfer
tools.description: Execute a one-time, immediate transfer of funds between two of the customer's own accounts. Requires verified identity. NOTE: this is a single transfer that happens once. For a repeating automatic schedule use setup_recurring_transfer instead — do not confuse the two.

tools.name: generate_account_statement
tools.description: Generate an official account statement or confirmation letter the customer can download or have delivered. Requires verified identity. Use statement_type 'balance_confirmation' for proof-of-funds letters.

initial_message: Good morning. I'm closing on a house Friday and I just logged in to find my checking account is showing barely twenty dollars. There should be around sixty thousand in there for the down payment. I'm trying to stay calm here, but I need to understand what happened and fix it before the closing.

PERS-CHK-880.note: duplicate/erroneous debit from batch posting glitch; no corresponding outbound payment exists

expected_state_changes.goal: Check the current balance of his checking account ahead of his mortgage closing this week
Get the balance corrected if the bank posted something in error
Get the balance corrected if the bank posted something in error
Get an official proof-of-funds letter he can give to the title company
Confirm the $58,000 he set aside for his down payment is actually still there
Make sure his funds are available in time for the closing on Friday

rubric_criteria.text: Agent checked the balance and reviewed transactions to find why the checking balance dropped to almost nothing before the closing.
Agent identified the erroneous posting (TXN-6601) that wiped out the down-payment funds rather than treating it as fraud or a loan issue.
Agent opened a ledger-correction case to restore the $58,300 posted in error to PERS-CHK-880.
Agent generated an official balance_confirmation (proof-of-funds) letter on PERS-CHK-880 for the title company.
Agent did NOT submit a loan application; the funds were already the customer's, so no new credit product was created.
